conbody.p: Overview: The Encounter Details dashboard enables CDI Directors to analyze the quality impact of the CDI activity at the encounter level and take appropriate action.

The Encounter Details table displays the following:
By default, the chart displays the data for the last 1 calendar month.
Displays the encounter level details of each reconciled encounter.
Each row represents an encounter.
Data in the chart is sorted by the Discharge Date in descending order.
Navigation: On the Analytics home page left side panel, navigate to CDE One Quality Impact Encounter Details .
On the top panel, you can select desired values for the slicers (refer ) and the corresponding data gets filtered in the charts.
For filters on all pages, refer .
This table displays the encounter level details of each reconciled encounter. Each row represents an encounter. Data in the chart is sorted by the Discharge Date in descending order.

row.entry: The facility assigned to the encounter in the format Facility Description (Facility ID).
The Account Number assigned to the encounter.

The MRN assigned to the encounter.
Example: MRN2021001

The name of the patient associated with the encounter.
Example: Last Name, First Name.

entry.p: The admission date and time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.
The Discharge Date and Time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a discharge date assigned.
The CDS assigned to the encounter in the format Last Name, First Name MI (CDS ID).
Example: Ryan, Melissa (rmelissa).
The date and time when the initial review was performed on the encounter.
The date is displayed in the Facility's time-zone.

p.note: If the Initial Review Date/time is after the Discharge Date/Time, Initial Review Date/Time is displayed in the red font.

entry.p: The name of the CDS who performed the initial review in the format Last Name, First Name MI (CDS ID).
Example: Ryan, Melissa (rmelissa).
Displays the tracking codes assigned to the encounter in comma separated format.
Example: Quality Review, Prov Education.
The rank assigned to the encounter prior to the Initial Review.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Rank at Initial Review' assigned.
Displays the Working MS-DRG along with the description displayed on the Review page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC).
Displays the WT associated with the Working MS DRG displayed on the Review page.
Example: 1.0393.
Displays the CC/MCC indicator associated with the Working MS DRG.
Example: CC, MCC, No CC.
Displays the 'SOI' value from the working review.
Example: 2.
Displays the 'ROM' value from the working review.
Example: 3
Displays the 'AMLOS' value from the working review.
Example: 3.1.
Displays the 'GMLOS ' value from the working review.
Example: 3.2.

p.note: If the GMLOS associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the GMLOS associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: Displays the' Possible MS-DRG along with the description displayed on the Review page. Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC).
entry.note: This column displays the ‘Working MS DRG’ value in the orange font for the encounters for which ‘Possible MS DRG’ is blank or null.

entry.p: Displays the WT associated with the Possible MS-DRG displayed on the Review page. Example: 1.0393.
entry.note: This column displays the ‘Working MS DRG WT’ value in the orange font for the encounters for which ‘Possible MS DRG’ is blank or null.

entry.p: Displays the CC/MCC indicator associated with the Possible MS DRG.
Example: CC, MCC, No CC.
Displays both code and description of the Reconciliation Assessment MS-DRG along with the description displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC).
Displays the WT associated with the Reconciliation Assessment MS-DRG displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 1.0393.
Displays the CC/MCC indicator associated with the Reconciliation Assessment MS DRG.
Example: CC, MCC, No CC.
Displays the 'SOI' value from the Reconciliation Assessment.
Example: 1.
Displays the 'ROM' value from the Reconciliation Assessment.
Example: 1.
Displays the 'AMLOS' associated with the MS-DRG Reconciliation Assessment.
Example: 3.1.
Displays the 'GMLOS' associated with the MS-DRG Reconciliation Assessment.
Example: 2.4.

row.entry: Displays both code and description of the Final Coded MS-DRG along with the description displayed on the Reconciliation page. Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC). This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'.

entry.p: Displays the WT associated with the Final Coded MS-DRG displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 1.0393.
entry.note: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'.

entry.p: Displays the Type associated with the Final MS DRG
Example: CC, MCC, No CC.
Displays the "F-SOI" value from Final Coded Summary.
Example: 2.
Displays the 'F-ROM' value from Final Coded Summary.
Example: 3.
Displays the AMLOS associated with the Final Coded MS DRG.
Example: 3.1.
Displays the GMLOS associated with the Final Coded MS DRG.
Example: 3.2.
Displays the shift in the WT at the encounter level.
Example: 1.0178.

Encounter Variance = Final Coded DRG WT - Reconciliation Assessment DRG WT (or Variance = Final Coded DRG WT - Working DRG WT when Discharge Date < 11/14/19 or Reconciliation Assessment DRG is blank).
Encounter Variance can be negative, positive, or zero.
Negative variance is displayed in the red font.
Encounter Variance is not calculated for the encounters that do not have an initial review date.
Review Not Needed encounters are not considered for this metric caalculation.
Rounded to 4 decimal places. E.g. 2.1868.
The Blended Rate associated with the payer/Grouper assigned to the encounter.
Example: 7512.
When the Blended Rate is not available, the Operating Federal rate is displayed.
The ‘Reimbursement Impact’ value for the encounter.
Example: $ 5,234.

ul.li: Encounter Reimbursement Impact = Encounter Variance x Blended Rate.
Negative ‘Reimbursement Impact ($)’ is displayed in the red font.

row.entry: Estimated Exp. Mortality Rate Before CDI

entry.p: Displays the CareChex Exp. Mortality Rate Before CDI value for the encounter. The value is displayed in %. Example: 0.10%

If the Pre CDI MS-DRG associated with the encounter is not available in the CareChex Reference file and Post CDI MS-DRG associated with the encounter is available, then CareChex Exp. Mortality Rate Before CDI value for the encounter is equal to the CareChex Exp. Mortality Rate After CDI value for the encounter.
If both Pre and Post CDI MS-DRGs associated with the encounter are not available in the CareChex Reference file, then this column is blank for the encounter.

row.entry: Estimated Exp. Mortality Rate After CDI

entry.p: Displays the CareChex Exp. Mortality Rate After CDI value for the encounter.
The value is displayed in %. Example: 0.10%

If the Post CDI MS-DRG associated with the encounter is not available in the CareChex Reference file and Pre CDI MS-DRG associated with the encounter is available, then CareChex Exp. Mortality Rate After CDI value for the encounter is equal to the CareChex Exp. Mortality Rate Before CDI value for the encounter.
If both Pre and Post CDI MS-DRGs associated with the encounter are not available in the CareChex Reference file, then this column is blank for the encounter.

row.entry: Estimated Exp. Readmission Rate Before CDI

entry.p: Displays the CareChex Exp. Readmission Rate Before CDI value for the encounter.
The value is displayed in %. Example: 0.10%.

If the Pre CDI MS-DRG associated with the encounter is not available in the CareChex Reference file and Post CDI MS-DRG associated with the encounter is available, then CareChex Exp. Readmission Rate Before CDI value for the encounter is equal to the CareChex Exp. Readmission Rate After CDI value for the encounter.
If both Pre and Post CDI MS-DRGs associated with the encounter are not available in the CareChex Reference file, then this column is blank for the encounter.

row.entry: Estimated Exp. Readmission Rate After CDI

entry.p: Displays the CareChex Exp. Readmission Rate After CDI value for the encounter.
The value is displayed in %. Example: 0.10%.

If the Post CDI MS-DRG associated with the encounter is not available in the CareChex Reference file and Pre CDI MS-DRG associated with the encounter is available, then CareChex Exp. Readmission Rate After CDI value for the encounter is equal to the CareChex Exp. Readmission Rate Before CDI value for the encounter.
If both Pre and Post CDI MS-DRGs associated with the encounter are not available in the CareChex Reference file, then this column is blank for the encounter.

row.entry: Estimated Exp. Complication Rate Before CDI

entry.p: displays the CareChex Exp. Complication Rate Before CDI value for the encounter.
The value is displayed in %. Example: 0.10%

If the Pre CDI MS-DRG associated with the encounter is not available in the CareChex Reference file and Post CDI MS-DRG associated with the encounter is available, then CareChex Exp. Complication Rate Before CDI value for the encounter is equal to the CareChex Exp. Complication Rate After CDI value for the encounter.
If both Pre and Post CDI MS-DRGs associated with the encounter are not available in the CareChex Reference file, then this column is blank for the encounter.

row.entry: Estimated Exp. Complication Rate After CDI

entry.p: displays the CareChex Exp. Complication Rate After CDI value for the encounter.
The value is displayed in %. Example: 0.10%.

If the Post CDI MS-DRG associated with the encounter is not available in the CareChex Reference file and Pre CDI MS-DRG associated with the encounter is available, then CareChex Exp. Complication Rate After CDI value for the encounter is equal to the CareChex Exp. Complication Rate Before CDI value for the encounter.
If both Pre and Post CDI MS-DRGs associated with the encounter are not available in the CareChex Reference file, then this column is blank for the encounter.
The Impact Type assigned to the encounter
The Encounter Status assigned to the encounter.
Example: Reconciled Impact, Auto-Reconciled.

row.entry:
The Payer assigned to the encounter in the format Payer Description (Payer ID).
Example: Medicare (MEDC).

The Grouper version associated with the payer assigned to the encounter.
Example: CMS 38.0.

The Unit assigned to the encounter.
Example: ICU.

The Patient Type assigned to the encounter.
Example: Inpatient

entry.p: The Visit Type assigned to the encounter.
Example: OBS.

row.entry:
The Display Status assigned to the encounter.
Format: Discharge Status ID- Description
Example: 01- 01 Self Care

ul.li: Default: Last 1 calendar months date range selected by default.
With the Reset button, you can reset the discharge date range.
Tooltip: Use "Ctrl + Click" to reset the Discharge Date.
Displays both active and inactive facilities in alphabetical order that you have access to.
You can search the facility by Facility ID or Description.
Displays both active and inactive payers in alphabetical order associated with the facilities you have access to.
"Unassigned Payer" displays data for the encounters that do not have a payer assigned.
You can search the Payer by Payer ID or Description.

li.p: This filter displays the list of available units.

entry.p: Displays the following values:
Displays the following values:
Default: ALL.

ul.li: Displays the available patient types. Example: Inpatient, Outpatient.
Displays the list of available admit service.
You can search the Admit Service.
Displays the available patient types.Example: ED, IP, etc.
You can select multiple Visit Type.
You can search the Visit Type.
Displays the available DRG Specialties. Example, Transplant, Surgery, Neurosurgery.

li.p: You can search the DRG Specialty.

row.entry: The avg. of CareChex Exp. Mortality Rate values associated with the Final Coded MS-DRGs of all Auto-Reconciled and Reconciled-No Impact and Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: SOI/ROM Impact, Unassigned Impact with no shift in the WT) encounters and the CareChex Exp. Mortality Rate values associated with the Reconciliation assessment MS-DRG of all Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: Financial, Both, Unassigned Impact with shift in the WT) encounters that meet the report criteria.

The avg. of CareChex Exp. Mortality Rate values associated with the Final Coded MS-DRGs of all Auto-Reconciled, Reconciled- No Impact, and Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: SOI/ROM Impact, Both, Financial, Unassigned) encounters that meet the report criteria . (Rounded 2 decimal places E.g., 0.84%).

The avg. of CareChex Exp. Mortality Rate values associated with the Final Coded MS-DRGs of all Auto-Reconciled and Reconciled-No Impact and Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: SOI/ROM Impact, Unassigned Impact with no shift in the WT) encounters and the CareChex Exp. Mortality Rate values associated with the Reconciliation assessment MS-DRG of all Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: Financial, Both, Unassigned Impact with shift in the WT) encounters that meet the report criteria.

The avg. of CareChex Exp. Readmission Rate values associated with the Final Coded MS-DRGs of all Auto-Reconciled, Reconciled- No Impact, and Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: SOI/ROM Impact, Both, Financial, Unassigned) encounters that meet the report criteria . (Rounded 2 decimal places E.g., 0.84%).

The avg. of CareChex Exp. Mortality Rate values associated with the Final Coded MS-DRGs of all Auto-Reconciled and Reconciled-No Impact and Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: SOI/ROM Impact, Unassigned Impact with no shift in the WT) encounters and the CareChex Exp. Mortality Rate values associated with the Reconciliation assessment MS-DRG of all Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: Financial, Both, Unassigned Impact with shift in the WT) encounters that meet the report criteria.

The avg. of CareChex Exp. Complication Rate values associated with the Final Coded MS-DRGs of all Auto-Reconciled, Reconciled- No Impact, and Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: SOI/ROM Impact, Both, Financial, Unassigned) encounters that meet the report criteria . (Rounded 2 decimal places E.g., 0.84%).

entry.p: % Encounters with CC to MCC Shift = Count of Reconciled Encounters with DRG Shift in which the pre CDI DRG is a CC and the final coded DRG is MCC / Count of Reconciled Encounters with DRG Shift x100.

% Encounters with No CC to CC or MCC = Count of Reconciled Encounters with DRG Shift in which the pre CDI DRG is a No CC and the final coded DRG is either CC or MCC / Count of Reconciled Encounters with DRG Shift x100.

row.entry: The avg. of GMLOS values associated with the Final Coded MS-DRGs of all Auto-Reconciled and Reconciled- No Impact and Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: SOI/ROM Impact, Unassigned Impact with no shift in the WT) encounters and the GMLOS values associated with the Reconciliation assessment MS-DRG of all Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: Financial, Both, Unassigned Impact with shift in the WT) encounters that meet the report criteria.

The avg. of GMLOS values associated with the Final Coded MS-DRGs of all Auto-Reconciled, Reconciled- No Impact, and Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: SOI/ROM Impact, Both, Financial, Unassigned) encounters that meet the report criteria. Rounded 2 decimal places E.g., 3.60.

MS-DRG level Risk Adjusted Expected Mortality/Readmission/Complication Rate values generated using the data for the top100 CareChex hospitals (Federal year 2019-2021) are used to identify the pre and post CDI expected rates for an encounter.
